workspace.video.promptInput.placeholder: Example: At dusk by the sea, a girl jogs along a damp wooden boardwalk. The camera tracks low, then slowly pulls back as the wind lifts the edge of her jacket; cinematic with warm golden light.
workspace.video.promptInput.promptTip.co: Start with the subject, scene, and motion, then add lighting, mood, or camera language. Results automatically return to Project Knowledge.
workspace.video.promptInput.shortcut.con: Press Enter to generate, Shift + Enter for a new line.
workspace.video.promptInput.title: Describe the scene, camera, and pacing you want to generate
workspace.video.canvas.imageInsert.succe: Set as the video start reference image
workspace.video.sidebar.intro.title: Generation parameters
workspace.video.sidebar.intro.tipContent: Choose the model first, then add reference images and output specs. Keep this panel lightweight while the main creation stays on the canvas.
workspace.video.sidebar.helper.title: Creation tips
workspace.video.sidebar.helper.prompt.co: Prioritize the subject, scene, camera motion, and lighting in the prompt. Successful videos sync automatically into Project Knowledge for reuse.
workspace.video.sidebar.helper.parameter: Lock the model and aspect ratio first, then add reference images or fixed-camera constraints gradually so each result stays easy to judge.
Use it to lock the opening composition, subject, and scene mood.
workspace.video.sidebar.reference.end.ti: Use it to constrain the final shot so the beginning and ending stay coherent.
workspace.video.sidebar.reference.replac: Drag and drop to replace the current image
Drag or click to upload a reference image
workspace.video.sidebar.controls.duratio: Start with 4 to 8 seconds to verify the shot, then extend gradually.
workspace.video.sidebar.controls.seed.ti: Lock the seed only when you need to reproduce a result; keep it random while exploring.
workspace.video.sidebar.controls.generat: Turn this on only when you need ambience or a simple soundtrack.
workspace.video.sidebar.controls.cameraF: Reduce camera movement for product shots or static scenes.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.tipContent: Model capabilities determine the available resolutions, durations, and image-to-video support.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.panel.desc: Review model capabilities, cost, and provider source in one panel so the sidebar stays focused.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.empty.desc: Configure a provider that supports video generation first.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.meta.veo31: Google Veo 3.1 supports 1080p/4K, multi-image references, and first/last frames.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.meta.sora2: Sora-2 Pro takes about 2 minutes to generate and offers higher stability.
Sora 2 supports up to 15 seconds and does not support uploading person images.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.meta.seeda: Supports text-to-video and start-frame / start-end-frame image-to-video.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.meta.minim: Supports text-to-video and image-to-video for general-purpose scenes.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.meta.runwa: Image-to-video only; best when you already have a visual anchor.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.meta.seeda: Lightweight Seedance variant with faster speed and lower cost.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.meta.wanx2: Alibaba Wanxiang keyframe image-to-video Plus model.
workspace.video.sidebar.model.meta.fallb: Capabilities and billing depend on the provider console.

workspace.video.workspace.hero.tipConten: Start video generation with one clear scene description, then add camera movement, emotion, and visual anchors. Get the structure working first, then layer in reference images and parameter constraints.
workspace.video.workspace.summary.curren: Select a video provider from the left panel first.
